title: Hegseth asks U.S. Army’s top officer to step down while U.S. continues war on Iran
description: Pentagon officials are not offering a reason for George's departure, which comes nearly five weeks into U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran and with no clear timeline from President Donald Trump on when the war may end.

title: Trump unveils 100% tariff on some patented drugs on ’Liberation Day’ anniversary
description: Companies that have signed a “most favoured nation” pricing deal and are actively building facilities in the U.S. to onshore production of patented pharmaceuticals and their ingredients will have a 0% tariff

title: Kerala’s development strategy highlights green growth, but allocations fall short | Data
description: In the war setting, the goal becomes to protect politically sensitive sectors and to dilute long-term public goods. But scientific research and environmental protection in particular lack organised constituencies even as their costs are immediate

title: Ajinkya Rahane lashes out at critics over strike-rate talk amid KKR’s loss to SRH: ‘They don’t like to watch me play’
How the Yamuna appeared relatively ‘cleaner’ during Chhath Puja in Delhi last year
description: Last year, just before the start of Chhath Puja, excess water discharged into the holy river from Hathnikund Barrage in Haryana diluted the pollutants and made the water appear relatively ‘cleaner’ in Delhi. This excess discharge was stopped towards the end of the festivities causing the water quality to return to hazardous levels.

title: NGT dismisses application challenging Musi River Front Project
ECI transfer controversy, top court’s clarifications
description: Article 324 does not give unfettered powers to the Election Commission of India

title: 2021 fire: Jharkhand High Court says burn injuries health crisis in India, issues 120-day ultimatum to state for special units
Inside ‘The Beast’: How Trump fired Pam Bondi during a Supreme Court ride amid Epstein Files row
Drowning in monsoon, dreaming of growth: recurring monsoon flooding in Warangal
description: Warangal’s double reality is hard to miss. Recurring monsoon flooding continues to expose civic deficiencies even as a wave of big-ticket projects signals a push for growth. A 24-storey multi super-specialty hospital, the Kakatiya Mega Textile Park, Railway Manufacturing Unit, Mamnoor Airport and a newly sanctioned underground drainage system together reflect an infrastructure- and industry-led transformation under way in this key north Telangana city, reports P. Laxma Reddy

title: How princess Mrinalini Kumari is bringing Lunavada palace decor to living rooms
description: After studying furniture design in Florence, Mrinalini Kumari launches Loonawara, which blends heritage, wildlife and design with home decor

title: Sanjay Gupta reacts to Ranbir Kapoor starrer 'Ramayana' makers claim about VFX
description: Filmmaker Sanjay Gupta critiqued the 'Ramayana' teaser, questioning the hype around its Oscar-winning VFX team, emphasizing that technicians, not companies, receive accolades.  His earlier 'Khoda pahaad… nikla chuha' comment also drew parallels to the teaser's mixed reception, with some viewers deeming the visuals disappointing. Read on to know more in detail.

title: State has enough resources, subsidies to continue: Cheema
Oil prices today: Crude jumps beyond $111 as Trump dashes hopes of quick Iran war end
description: Middle East tensions escalated Friday, sending oil prices soaring as US strikes on Iran intensified. Brent crude climbed 7.8% to $109.03 per barrel, with WTI futures reaching record premiums. Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil transit route, are fueling supply concerns and pushing energy prices to multi-year highs.

title: Pakistan petrol price breaches PKR 450 as Islamabad effects massive hike amid West Asia war

title: Delhi HC directs takedown of ‘obscene’ song by Honey Singh, Badshah

description: None of the observatories in the city recorded any rainfall activity, India Meteorological Department scientist Sushma Nair said

US-born child’s ‘deep roots’ in India outweigh American court orders, Delhi High Court rules in bitter custody battle

description: Fresh explosions were reported in the Tehran area, with Iranian state television reporting U.S.-Israeli strikes hit a bridge in the northern town of Karaj twice -- the first causing civilian casualties and the second striking as emergency teams responded.

title: No sign of tribunal on promised Day 1 of hearings, Bengal CEO says: ‘I can’t say when it will start’
Government puts on hold its order asking airlines to offer 60 per cent free seating
Parliament passes Jan Vishwas Amendment Bill
description: Out of 784, 717 provisions are being amended for decriminalising minor offences and 67 to facilitate ease of living

title: Column | Krithi Karanth’s rules of co-existence
description: The conservation biologist on why children are key to building awareness of human-animal interactions

title: From selfies to slogan: how Instagram is becoming India’s newest political battleground
description: In the run-up to Assembly elections this month, social media is flooded with viral memes and AI-generated disinformation, and it is reshaping political speech, influence and visibility online

title: Flutter over grading third language
description: Grading the third-language paper and not counting it towards the total marks in SSLC in Karnataka is being seen as the first step towards a two-language policy, writes Jayanth R.

title: Why Raghav Chadha lost AAP’s trust: Inside the disconnect between party, Arvind Kejriwal’s former protégé
India’s crude price hits 4-year high; OPEC mulls hike in output
Review | Ali Smith pairs imagination with urgency in her politically charged new novel, Glyph
description: Pay attention to the history you are making in the present, Smith suggests, or it will return to haunt you

title: Hyderabad techie dies by suicide over 'dowry harassment'
description: A 26-year-old software professional from Bihar allegedly died by suicide in Hyderabad, found by her husband. Police have taken the husband into custody, citing alleged mental harassment for dowry and pressure for investment in his startup. The couple, married in February after a five-year live-in relationship, reportedly faced marital discord.

title: Char Dham yatra ready? NGT orders GPS on tankers to save Ganga from sewage
Artemis II astronauts rocket toward moon after spending day around Earth
description: NASA had the Artemis II crew stick close to home for a day to test their capsule's life-support systems before clearing them for lunar departure; now committed to the moon, the Artemis II test flight is the opening act for NASA's grand plans for a moon base and sustained lunar living.

title: 33 days, no answers: Family of India’s first West Asia war casualty moves Bombay HC to bring sailor’s body home
Madhya Pradesh MLA made ‘attempt to call’ judge hearing mining case, High Court initiates contempt action
No handshake row hits FIDE Candidates 2026 - Watch
description: Ukraine’s Anna Muzychuk defeated Russia’s Kateryna Lagno in Round 4 of the Women’s Candidates 2026, with no handshake or acknowledgement reflecting ongoing geopolitical tensions. Muzychuk’s precise play exploited Lagno’s critical error on move 28, winning in 31 moves. The victory puts Muzychuk atop the leaderboard with 2.5 points, tied with Bibisara Assaubayeva.

title: Trump announces destruction of Iran's tallest bridge
description: Trump posted footage on social media of smoke rising from the B1 bridge in Karaj, around 35 km southwest of Tehran — and warned that there would be further destruction unless Iran comes to the table to end the five-week war

title: Hegseth asks US Army’s top uniformed officer to step down amid Iran war
Hema Malini writes to Speaker; says West Bengal denying permission for cultural events
description: “I have written a letter to the Honourable Speaker, highlighting a cultural fascism in West Bengal and how it is adversely affecting several people from the cultural world,” the Mathura MP said in a post on X

title: Education Ministry notifies NCERT as deemed university
description: The notification dated March 30 enables the NCERT to offer courses and confer degrees; it says all such academic programmes must conform to UGC norms and standards

BJP alleges ‘lawlessness’ in West Bengal over Malda incident, calls for Mamata’s resignation
description: Party spokesperson Gaurav Bhatia said ‘jungle raj’ was prevailing in the State under Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee

title: 'Agenda against me, they’re jealous’: Rahane's no-holds-barred swipe at critics
description: Ajinkya Rahane fiercely defended his batting and leadership after Kolkata Knight Riders' heavy defeat to Sunrisers Hyderabad. He dismissed critics questioning his strike rate, claiming they have agendas and are jealous of his success. Despite KKR's collapse, Rahane insisted his intent was clear and backed his approach, remaining defiant amidst mounting pressure.

description: The HSBC India Manufacturing Index noted that the new orders and output for manufacturing firms rose at their slowest rates since mid-2022

description: The plea termed ‘racial and social discrimination’ the revocation of the quota granted under the Socially and Educationally Backward Classes category

description: The High Court had on November 14, 2025 directed the State government to hold these polls by April 15

title: Arti Singh recalls escape: Actor says she jumped from second floor; later faced panic attacks
description: Bigg Boss 13 fame Arti Singh bravely recounted a harrowing childhood experience. At just 13, she narrowly escaped a sexual assault attempt by a house helper. In a desperate bid for freedom, she jumped from the second floor of her home. This traumatic event, which she previously shared on Bigg Boss 13, led to panic attacks.

title: Pakistan says onus on Afghanistan to end hostilities
description: The neighbours and one-time allies have been locked in an escalating conflict over claims from Islamabad that Afghanistan is harbouring militants responsible for cross-border attacks, which the Taliban government denies

title: Trump fires Pam Bondi as U.S. attorney general, White House official says
description: U.S. President Donald Trump had also reportedly grown frustrated that Ms. Bondi was not moving quickly enough ​to prosecute critics and adversaries who he wanted to face criminal charges

title: Body transformation specialist Kinita Kadakia Patel shares celebrity secrets, says Jimmy Shergill displays ‘unmatched discipline’

Explained: Right to be considered for promotion as a fundamental right, and how it works in practice
IPL 2026: Abhishek Sharma scripts history, joins David Warner to....
description: Abhishek Sharma achieved a significant milestone for Sunrisers Hyderabad, becoming the second player to hit 100 sixes for the franchise. SRH secured their first IPL 2026 win by defeating Kolkata Knight Riders by 65 runs, defending a season-high total of 226/8 with a strong bowling performance.

title: Bengaluru saw over 2,100 new vehicles a day on average; total count touches 1.2 crore
description: Bengaluru's vehicle count surpassed 1.2 crore by March 2026, with over 7.9 lakh new vehicles added in 2025-26, straining the city's infrastructure. This surge, driven by private vehicle dependence due to inadequate public transport, exacerbates congestion and poses public health risks. Experts urge a policy shift towards promoting public transport.

title: ‘Dhurandhar 2' BO Day 15: Ranveer starrer inches close to Rs 1,500cr worldwide
description: Ranveer Singh's spy thriller 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' has crossed Rs 1490 crore worldwide by the end of its second week. The film is now poised to surpass Rs 1500 crore globally as it enters its third week. Domestically, the net collection is nearing Rs 950 crore, with gross business aiming for Rs 1130 crore.

title: Nepal’s ruling Rashtriya Swatantra Party asks ‘political appointees’ to leave public offices
Why Bihar govt letter on Z-plus security for Nitish Kumar irks his JDU colleagues
Singapore seeks bigger stake in UP: ‘Investment roadmap being made’
Relief likely for UP madrasa teachers hit by scheme withdrawal
Will camp in Bengal for 15 days… people waiting for parivartan, says Shah
‘Jungle raj in state’: BJP demands TMC minister Sabina Yeasmin’s arrest over Malda incident
Everest climbers ‘poisoned’ in $20m insurance scam, probe on
description: Nepal's Everest climbing season begins amidst a massive insurance fraud investigation. Authorities are probing a scheme involving guides, helicopter operators, and hospital staff accused of faking medical emergencies to claim nearly $20 million.  Over 300 fake rescues are suspected, with 32 individuals charged. This scandal threatens international insurer confidence in Nepal's rescue operations.

title: Several minority scholarship schemes discontinued after 2021-22, Minority Affairs Minister tells Rajya Sabha
description: Mr. Brittas has also asked about the total scholarship amount allocated and actually given each year over the last five years, State-wise along with the details of the scholarship schemes for minorities which were stopped or merged in the last five years

title: Parliament to reconvene for women quota bills on April 16
23 years later, spotlight back on Chhattisgarh’s ‘first political killing’
Raghav Chadha dropped as AAP deputy RS leader, replaced by Ashok Mittal
description: In a surprising leadership shake-up, the Aam Aadmi Party has appointed Ashok Mittal as the new Rajya Sabha deputy leader, effectively taking over from Raghav Chadha. This move hints at underlying tensions between Chadha and the party hierarchy. His noticeable absence from pivotal moments, such as Arvind Kejriwal's recent legal tumult, has sparked conjecture about his diminishing influence.

title: Violence-hit Malda BDO deserted as Bengal protests continue, as do queues of applicants
Will MS Dhoni play today? Viral training clip excites fans ahead of CSK vs PBKS clash
description: MS Dhoni has been spotted training ahead of Chennai Super Kings' IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings, fueling hopes of an earlier-than-expected return from a calf strain. The veteran captain missed CSK's opening match defeat, but his presence at practice sessions has boosted fan optimism.

title: Telangana food safety inspections uncover hygiene violations, 146 kg unsafe meat seized
description: According to officials, a total of 68 inspections were carried out under the State-wide meat drive. During these checks, 33 samples were collected for analysis and 56 notices were issued to food business operators for various violations

title: 40 petro products exempted from customs duty for 3 months
description: To alleviate the burden of soaring input costs on various industries, the government has enacted a three-month suspension of customs duties on 40 key petrochemical products. This initiative is focused on providing support to sectors including plastics, packaging, and textiles.

title: India flags ‘unimpeded transit’, 60-nation meet calls for reopening Hormuz
Anime to activism: Netflix’s One Piece Straw Hat flag becomes a global protest symbol
description: How the Straw Hat went from Eiichiro Oda’s best-selling One Piece manga to the Netflix’s hit series to fuelling a new language of rebellion worldwide

title: CPWD officer held for graft in WHO, BSF facilities’ construction
Governance today needs new capabilities: Mishra
Cancer 10th leading cause of death among kids in India: Study
Defence exports skyrocket to record Rs 38,424cr in 2025-26, 62.7% rise over previous fiscal
description: India's defence exports have soared to an unprecedented Rs 38,424 crore in FY 2025-26, a remarkable 62.7% surge. This achievement underscores growing global confidence in India's indigenous defence manufacturing capabilities. Major exports include missile systems, artillery, and aircraft, reaching over 80 countries, solidifying India's position as a significant global defence exporter.

title: Chhattisgarh high court finds Amit Jogi guilty in 2003 murder case, asks him to surrender in 3 weeks
description: The Chhattisgarh High Court has overturned the acquittal of former legislator Amit Jogi in the 23-year-old murder case of NCP leader Ram Avatar Jaggi. The court found Jogi guilty and ordered him to surrender within three weeks. This verdict comes after the CBI appealed Jogi's earlier acquittal by a trial court due to alleged lack of evidence.

title: Parliament to reconvene on April 16 for 3 days; we have certain Bills, says Rijiju
Ahmedabad Metro to run  till 12:50 am on 7 IPL match days, Rs 50 per ticket
Any misadventure from Pak to invite ‘unprecedented’ action, says Rajnath Singh
description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh firmly addressed the situation with Pakistan, asserting that any further provocations would prompt a reaction that would be both 'unprecedented and decisive.' He shared that Operation Sindoor brought India mere moments away from a significant sea strike, effectively incapacitating Pakistan's terror apparatus in a swift 22 minutes after the attack in Pahalgam.

title: NDD ban: Is it the end of speculative games in rupee?
Omar Abdullah demands probe into Ganderbal encounter, after family claims ‘slain had no militant links’
description: “I believe the claim of the family should not be dismissed out of hand. At the very least this encounter needs a transparent and time bound probe with the facts made public. Any attempt to obfuscate or delay the announcement of a probe will only damage credibility & that is not in anyone’s interest,” says Mr. Abdullah

title: Compulsory third language in Class 6, vocational education a must in Class 9-10: CBSE releases new curriculum
Astronaut Shukla lauds Artemis II launch, recalls meeting crew
description: Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla celebrated the Artemis II Moon mission launch, drawing parallels to his own experiences at Kennedy Space Center. He highlighted the historic significance of the launch pad, echoing Neil Armstrong's legacy. Shukla expressed pride in witnessing the astronauts' journey, underscoring India's growing collaboration with NASA through the Artemis Accords and its independent lunar ambitions.

title: Congress delegation meets Maha Chief Electoral Officer, demands impartial SIR
description: “The complaints that voters belonging to specific castes and religions were deliberately excluded, large-scale irregularities have been reported in SIR drives that might have benefited the ruling party,” said State Congress president Harshwardhan Sapkal

title: Opposition flags timing of women’s quota bill changes; government rejects charge
description: Opposition parties in the Rajya Sabha accused the government of political manoeuvring by proposing amendments to the women's reservation law, alleging it's timed for the upcoming assembly elections. The government defended its right to decide legislative timing, emphasising a commitment to women's empowerment and dismissing election-related motives. A heated exchange ensued over the implementation timeline.

title: U.P. Government committed to ensuring every eligible and needy person receives benefits of welfare schemes, says CM Adityanath
description: “The government is committed to ensuring that every eligible and needy person receives the benefits of welfare schemes and every issue is resolved effectively,” says CM Adityanath

title: Russia’s First Dy PM meets Modi; talk trade ties boost
Delhi Mayor flags off flood control, sanitation fleet
description: Mr. Singh said residents would not witness the extent of waterlogging seen in previous years during the coming monsoon. “This is the second phase of induction of advanced machines into the MCD fleet,” says Delhi Mayor

title: ‘Law & order breakdown’: SC slams gheraoing in Bengal
description: The Supreme Court expressed grave concern over an attack on judicial officers in West Bengal's Malda, citing a breakdown in law and order.  The apex court has issued show-cause notices to top state officials and ordered central forces for officer protection. A central agency will investigate the 'deliberate and calculated' attempt to obstruct justice.

title: Lok Sabha passes CAPF Bill with voice vote; Opposition members protest, walk out
M.P. HC to begin regular hearing of Bhojshala-Kamal Maula mosque dispute case from April 6
description: The development comes a day after the Supreme Court refused to intervene in the matter on a petition from the Muslim side while directing the High Court to consider the objections of all parties on the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI)’s report of a scientific survey of the site

title: Sparring officials, disputed Bill, two ‘unheeded’ probes: Sukhu govt in knots over housing projects
The putrid mix of money and medical malpractice
description: A woman’s visit to a health centre in February over money she was allegedly owed for repeated egg harvesting has led the Maharashtra police to a network of illegal medical businesses operating across at least three States. Seven persons have been arrested and investigators have seized forged documents and hormonal injections from an accused’s residence, finds Chinmay Gaikwad

title: Kumkis help tackle a jumbo task in Chittoor
description: The saga of wild elephants raiding crops along forest fringes had been a frequent news story. Conventional responses—firecrackers, drums, or vehicles—soon proved ineffective. Then came the Forest Department’s Kumki initiative, which began playing a vital role in guiding wild herds back. Their camp life— bond with mahouts and kavadis, daily routines, strength, and care—captures the essence of this initiative, explores K. Umashanker

title: Jonathan Lucroy calls out Kelce brothers, stresses true MLB grind after workout remarks
description: Jonathan Lucroy responded strongly after Travis Kelce and Jason Kelce questioned baseball workouts on their podcast. He explained that MLB players face a long, daily season that demands endurance, not just short bursts of effort. Using his own experience and examples like Nolan Ryan, Lucroy showed why baseball training is built for consistency, not quick intensity.

title: J&K SIA files chargesheet against accused involved in harbouring LeT terrorists
description: J&K's State Investigating Agency has charged Sabar Hussain from Poonch for aiding terrorists responsible for the brutal killing of a government employee in Rajouri two years ago. Hussain allegedly provided shelter and support to Lashkar-e-Taiba/TRF militants involved in the fatal attack on Mohammed Razaq. Prima facie, offences under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act are established.

title: How two brothers built Rakkaudella Chocolates, Kerala’s single-origin bean-to-bar chocolate
description: Kuriachan Johnson and his brother Ouseppachan turned their entrepreneurial gamble into Rakkaudella Chocolates, a single-origin bean-to-bar brand sourcing cocoa from Idukki. After early resistance in Kerala, the brothers now manufacture seven tonnes monthly, with plans to expand across India and abroad

title: Akin to 2024 bypoll, DMK and PMK are set to lock horns yet again in Vikravandi constituency
description: The constituency consists of Vanniyars, Dalits, religious minorities and caste equations, like in other constituencies in Villupuram district, play a significant role in the final outcome here. Though the constituency has a strong presence of Vanniyars, the PMK has never won from here

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Constituency Watch – a compilation
description: Tune in to The Hindu’s Constituency Watch segment to follow our on-ground Assembly election coverage

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026 | interviews with political leaders: a compilation
description: Find all our exclusive interviews with political leaders here.

description: The Chief Minister said the honour places the Uttarakhand Police in the league of select police forces of the country that have received this recognition

description: The Supreme Court has asked the Election Commission to hand over the probe either to CBI or NIA

title: BJP planning to impose President’s Rule: Mamata
description: Seven judicial officers were gheraoed by locals at a BDO office at Mothabari in the State’s Malda District on April 1; the officials were rescued later by a huge contingent of police and security forces

title: An agricultural belt plagued by unfair trade practices at DPCs, lack of storage facilities
description: Farmers call for steps to build a permanent warehouse, implement schemes to boost groundwater level, and link Palar-Thenpennai rivers

title: France bans planned gathering of Muslims in Paris area, citing security risk
description: “At the request of the ‌Interior Minister Laurent Nunez, I have issued an order ‌prohibiting the 40th edition of ‌the ⁠Annual Meeting of Muslims of ⁠France, from April 3 to April 6 at the Paris–Le Bourget Exhibition Centre,” the ​Paris Police ‌Chief Patrice Faure said in a post on X

title: A path to peace in West Asia beyond Pakistan talks
description: India could lead a Global South initiative at the United Nations to mediate the West Asia war

title: CM Omar seeks probe in Ganderbal ‘encounter’ after family disputes ‘terrorist’ claim
description: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah has demanded a swift, transparent investigation into the killing of a man in an alleged encounter. The deceased's family claims he had no terror links and was preparing to join the Army. Authorities are urged to make facts public to maintain credibility amid grave allegations.

title: Double-deck road along the Munak Canal is stuck after Haryana Govt delays NOC
Delhi govt to set up animal welfare committees in all districts
A betrayal foretold: On the U.S. and NATO alliance
description: After Donald Trump’s taunts and threats, Europe will have to reimagine its security paradigm

title: Almost a month after man killed on Holi, Hastsal Colony celebrates Hanuman Jayanti amid massive police, CRPF and RAF presence
‘Prevalent problem’: RWAs in Gurgaon welcome HC order staying stilt+4 constructions
Iran claims IRGC Navy struck Oracle facility in Dubai, escalating tech-targeted attacks
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) claims it struck an Oracle data center in Dubai, following threats against US tech firms. This follows a reported attack on an Amazon cloud center in Bahrain. The IRGC warned employees of 18 US companies, including Microsoft and Google, to evacuate facilities, citing retaliation for attacks in Iran.

title: Navy escorting tankers from Strait of Hormuz, no dearth of fuel or gas in country, says defence minister
description: India assures ample fuel and gas supplies amidst West Asia conflict, with the Navy actively escorting tankers through the Strait of Hormuz. Defence Minister Rajnath Singh highlighted PM Modi's diplomatic efforts and the nation's preparedness. Meanwhile, the Navy Chief noted evolving maritime competition beyond energy to critical resources and data, emphasizing increased marine activity and potential infringements on sovereign rights.

title: Gujarat High Court: ‘Voter’s mandate cannot be nullified over minor issues in affidavit’
Case of alleged custodial torture: HC dismisses plea seeking contempt action against ex-Rajkot DCP
No more Bondi-ing: Trump fires his attorney general for Epstein stain
description: US President Donald Trump has dismissed Attorney General Pam Bondi, his second cabinet member in a month, citing mounting frustration over her handling of the Epstein files and broader performance issues.  Despite public praise, the move highlights Trump's demand for results beyond mere loyalty, leaving a lingering atmosphere of uncertainty within his administration.

title: ‘UCC enactment raises doubts about consistency with central laws’: Governor urged to withhold assent
Conspiracy to jail AAP workers before polls, Kejriwal tells Gujarat CM
Govt pushes expansion of PNG network in 110 high priority areas
Police stop burial of girl in West Delhi: Family says died of typhoid; post-mortem says ‘smothered’
Critical petrochemical products get full customs duty exemption
4 in 10 companies are posting ‘fake’ jobs: Inside the ‘ghost hiring’ trend and 3 ways to spot such a listing before you apply
Sachs warns of escalation, oil disruption in Iran conflict; calls for global action
description: As the conflict stretches into its second month, leading economist Jeffrey Sachs expresses grave concerns regarding the intensifying war and global instability. He denounces US President Trump and Israeli PM Netanyahu for defying expert counsel in initiating the conflict, a choice that could lead to dramatic hikes in oil prices and prompt Iran to reassess its nuclear program.

title: Gemma 4 debuts: Google's powerhouse AI hits servers and smartphones—Pichai, Hassabis weigh in
description: Google has unveiled Gemma 4, its most capable open-source AI model family, designed for broad accessibility from data centers to smartphones.  These models offer advanced reasoning, agentic workflows, and native image/video processing, with smaller versions optimized for offline mobile use.  Gemma 4 aims to empower developers globally with powerful, versatile AI tools.

title: Rajnath chairs IGoM meeting, emphasises constant vigil to ease West Asia war effect
No word on motion against CEC, match-fixing in broad daylight: TMC’s O’Brien

description: Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Kolkata Knight Riders by 65 runs, powered by Heinrich Klaasen’s steady 52 and strong support from Nitish Kumar Reddy. After a rapid start from Head and Abhishek, SRH post 226/8. KKR begin well but collapse due to poor coordination and wickets, getting bowled out for 161 despite Raghuvanshi’s 52 and Rinku’s effort.

description: In a bold address, President Trump cautioned Iran, calling for immediate negotiations following US airstrikes that struck a significant bridge just outside of Tehran and a prominent medical institution. This development heightens regional instability, hinting at a more aggressive approach from the United States.

Oracle layoffs: 6 AM email that laid off 10,000+ employees did not come from HR
description: Oracle has begun its largest-ever layoffs, impacting thousands globally without prior notice. Employees received termination emails early Tuesday, with immediate system access revocation.  The company's aggressive AI data center expansion is cited as a key driver for these cuts, which could affect up to 30,000 staff. Severance packages require signing documents first.

title: Stephen Curry, Ayesha Curry team up with $150bn mogul
description: Stephen Curry and Ayesha Curry have joined Warren Buffett in reviving his iconic charity lunch through a new auction titled “A Seat At The Table.” The initiative blends Buffett’s legacy in philanthropy with the Currys’ growing influence beyond basketball. With proceeds benefiting the Glide Foundation and Eat. Learn. Play. Foundation, the event aims to create meaningful change in underserved communities. Scheduled for June 24 in Omaha, the auction reflects a powerful collaboration focused on impact, purpose, and long-term community support.

title: Martina Navratilova warns Emma Raducanu after Miami Open withdrawal
description: Emma Raducanu’s coaching changes are back in focus after Martina Navratilova urged her to stay with one coach longer. Raducanu, however, prefers flexibility and wants to rediscover her natural game. With mixed results, recent injuries, and no full-time coach, she stands at a key moment. Her next decision could define how her career moves forward.

title: Stephen Curry opens up on struggles as Ayesha Curry faces criticism
description: ​Stephen Curry, the NBA legend, is considered one of the most popular athletes in the world because of his talent. While Stephen Curry’s achievements have inspired billions of fans around the world, the Golden State Warriors’ star player remains committed to his career. Amid this, Stephen Curry opens up about what kept him going throughout the years through all the failures.
